name: Id attribute value is unique
description: This rule checks that all `id` attribute values on a single page are unique.
applicability_text:
This rule applies to any `id` attribute whose value is not an empty string (`""`), specified on a [HTML or SVG element][].

**Note:** Elements that are neither [included in the accessibility tree][] nor [visible][] are still considered for this rule.
expectations: The value of the attribute is unique across all other `id` attributes specified on [HTML or SVG elements][html or svg element] that exist within the same [document tree](https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#document-trees) or [shadow tree](https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#shadow-trees) as the element on which the applicable `id` attribute is specified.

examples.name: Passed Example 1
examples.description: There is only one `id` attribute within the document context.

examples.name: Passed Example 2
examples.description: All `id` attributes are unique within the document context.
examples.code:
This is my first element
This is my second element

examples.name: Passed Example 3
examples.description: Two of the `id` attributes are the same (`my-elt`), but they are in different trees (the first one in the [document tree](https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#document-trees) and the second in the [shadow tree](https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#shadow-trees)).

examples.name: Passed Example 4
examples.description: Both `id` attributes are the same (`my-elt`), but they are in different [document trees](https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#document-trees) because the `iframe` is creating a new one.

examples.name: Failed Example 1
examples.description: The `id` attribute `label` is not unique among all `id` attributes in the document, resulting in a wrong [programmatic label][] on the input field.

examples.name: Failed Example 2
examples.description: The `id` attribute `label` is not unique among all `id` attributes in the document, resulting in a wrong [programmatic label][] on the input field.
examples.code: Name

examples.name: Failed Example 3
examples.description: The `id` attribute `label` is not unique among all `id` attributes in the document, resulting in a wrong [programmatic label][] on the input field. This rule still considers elements that are neither [included in the accessibility tree][] nor [visible][].
examples.code: Name City

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 1
examples.description: There is no `id` attribute in this document.

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 2
examples.description: The `xml:id` attribute is not considered by this rule.

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 3
examples.description: These `id` attributes have an empty value.

accessibility_support: There are no accessibility support issues known.
